title: Chopper Feature JSON v1
description: Optional extension that injects, overrides, or removes procs/files/stages from the base flow. Features are layered on top of the base in selected order. Stages are optional; when used, stage fields map directly to generated run script files and optionally to stack entries such as N (name), J (command), L (legal exit codes), D (dependency graph), I (inputs), and O (outputs). Stack file generation is optional; users can create stack files manually or use Chopper's optional generated output.

domain.description: Optional: domain this feature is intended for (e.g., my_domain). If present and the selected base declares a different domain, the validator emits `VW-04 feature-domain-mismatch` (warning; escalates to error in `--strict`). Features without this field are domain-agnostic and can be applied to any domain without a warning.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this feature provides

depends_on.description: Optional prerequisite feature names. Values must reference other Feature JSON `name` fields, not file paths. Use this to declare inter-feature dependency chains. Project-level validation should ensure prerequisite features are selected and appear earlier in project.features order.

metadata.description: Optional informational metadata. Chopper never evaluates these fields — they are documentation only. Preserved in audit artifacts and scan output.

owner.description: Team or individual responsible for this feature

tags.description: Tags for feature catalog filtering (e.g., signoff, dft, power)

wiki.description: URL to wiki page or documentation for this feature

related_ivars.description: Documentation-only: ivar references related to this feature

related_appvars.description: Documentation-only: appvar references related to this feature

include.description: Files or glob patterns to include (domain-relative, forward slashes). Glob patterns: '*' (any chars except /), '?' (one char except /), '**' (any dirs/subdirs). Examples: 'procs/*.tcl', 'reports/**', 'setup.tcl'. MUST NOT be empty if files.include is present.

exclude.description: Files or glob patterns to exclude (domain-relative, forward slashes). Supports same glob patterns as include. Excludes only prune wildcard-expanded candidates; literal include paths survive. MUST NOT be empty if files.exclude is present.

include.description: Additional proc-level includes from this feature. MUST NOT be empty if procedures.include is present.

exclude.description: Proc-level excludes from this feature. Prunes procs inside PROC_TRIM files (keep-set = all_procs(file) − PE). Does not remove explicit procedures.include entries from any source (emits VW-18 cross-source-pe-vetoed) and does not remove whole-file includes (emits VW-10 cross-source-fe-vetoed). Does not filter trace-expanded callees — trace is reporting-only and never auto-copies procs. MUST NOT be empty if procedures.exclude is present.

flow_actions.description: Ordered list of flow actions applied top-to-bottom during compilation. Stage-creation actions should be authored with real stack output in mind: `name` -> `N`, `command` -> `J`, `exit_codes` -> `L`, and `dependencies` -> `D`. MUST NOT be empty if flow_actions is present.

file.description: Domain-relative Tcl file path. Must not contain '..' traversal or double slashes.

procs.description: Proc names. MUST NOT be empty.

reference.description: Reference step string to insert before/after. Supports @n instance targeting (e.g., step.tcl@2).

name.description: Name of the new stage. Becomes the filename of the generated `<stage>.tcl` run script and optionally `N <name>` in a manually-created stack file.

reference.description: Reference stage name to insert before/after

load_from.description: Predecessor stage for data loading inside the generated `<stage>.tcl` script, typically via ivar(src_task). This is separate from the optional stack-file `D` dependency graph line.

dependencies.description: Optional parent task names for the optional stack dependency graph. Become the `D` line in a manually-created stack file.

exit_codes.description: Optional legal exit codes for the added stage. Become the `L` line in a manually-created stack file.

command.description: Optional execution command for the added stage. Becomes the `J` line in a manually-created stack file.

inputs.description: Optional input artifact markers. Become one or more `I` lines in a manually-created stack file.

outputs.description: Optional output artifact markers. Become one or more `O` lines in a manually-created stack file.

run_mode.description: Optional: Execution mode (serial or parallel). Translated into R <value> in the stack file. Since 2025.12.

language.description: Optional: Language of the stage. Set to 'python' if the stage uses Python. Default is tcl.

steps.description: Ordered list of step strings that Chopper writes into the generated `<stage>.tcl` run script.

reference.description: Step string to remove. Use @n for instance targeting (e.g., step.tcl@2).
Step string to replace. Use @n for instance targeting (e.g., step.tcl@2).

name.description: Unique stage name. Becomes the filename of the generated `<stage>.tcl` run script and optionally `N <name>` in a manually-created stack file.

load_from.description: Predecessor stage for data loading inside the generated `<stage>.tcl` script. This is separate from the optional stack-file `D` dependency graph line.

dependencies.description: Optional parent task names for the optional stack dependency graph. Become the `D` line in a manually-created stack file.

exit_codes.description: Optional legal exit codes. Become the `L` line in a manually-created stack file.

command.description: Optional execution command. Becomes the `J` line in a manually-created stack file.

inputs.description: Optional input artifact markers. Become one or more `I` lines in a manually-created stack file.

outputs.description: Optional output artifact markers. Become one or more `O` lines in a manually-created stack file.

steps.description: Ordered list of step strings that Chopper writes into the generated `<stage>.tcl` run script.
